title: JDBC driver - Problems with non-Latin characters
description:
I have implemented a plain Java bulk loader to initialize many tables in a Virtuoso relational DB. I am experiencing a problem related to the insertion of Unicode text. Running the program, when the following INSERT statement is executed,
INSERT INTO DB.PEST.V_SEM_PRODUCT_NAMES_MOD(PRODUCT_CODE,PRODUCT_NAME_ID,LANGUAGE_CD,PART,PRODUCT_NAM,SCIENTIFIC_NAME,PART_NAME) VALUES('0130010',9949,'LV',1,'EĻĻAS AUGU SĒKLAS UN AUGĻI',NULL,NULL)

the inserted row has all the non-Latin characters corrupted (see the attachment below).
With the same INSERT statement having other non-Latin characters I get the following run-time error.
virtuoso.jdbc4.VirtuosoException exception
virtuoso.jdbc4.VirtuosoException: SQ074: Line 1:
        at virtuoso.jdbc4.VirtuosoResultSet.process_result(VirtuosoResultSet.java:631)

Instead the execution of these INSERT statements in the Conductor Interactive SQL is successful.
If I remove as well the non-Latin characters from such INSERT the Java program runs correctly.
I use the JDBC driveris virtjdbc4_1.jar found in the following folder.
I use as well Virtuoso Open Source Edition (Column Store) (multi threaded)
Version 7.2.4.2.3217-pthreads as of Feb  3 2017
for windows
I made a small example (an Eclipse Java project) able to reproduce the issue executing one INSERT statement only.
With the SQL statement currently configured in the example I get the text inserted in the DB corrupted in the non-Latin characters.
Please find attached
-the .zip file with the built java project (rename its extension as .jar),
-the property file where the executed SQL statement is, together with the DB connection string (VirtuosoBulkLoader.txt - rename its extension as .properties).
-a zip file with the example Java project source code.
Instructions to reproduce the issue
Change the DB connection string in the property file and copy the file in the same folder where the example jar is.
Create a table in the DB with the following structure.
CREATE TABLE DB.PEST.V_SEM_PRODUCT_NAMES_MOD   -- DICTIONARY
(
  PRODUCT_CODE    VARCHAR(7) NOT NULL,
  PRODUCT_NAME_ID NUMERIC NOT NULL,
  LANGUAGE_CD VARCHAR(2) NOT NULL,
  PART NUMERIC NOT NULL,
  PRODUCT_NAM NVARCHAR(500),
  SCIENTIFIC_NAME NVARCHAR(500),
  PART_NAME NVARCHAR(500),
  CONSTRAINT PK_V_SEM_PRODUCT_NAMES_MOD PRIMARY KEY (PRODUCT_NAME_ID )
);

You can directly run the example with the following command.
java -jar .\virtJdbcDriverProb.jar


virtJdbcDriverProb.zip
sourceCode.zip
VirtuosoBulkLoader.txt
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE DB.PEST.V_SEM_PRODUCT_NAMES_MOD (PRODUCT_CODE VARCHAR(7) NOT NULL, PRODUCT_NAME_ID DECIMAL NOT NULL, LANGUAGE_CD VARCHAR(2) NOT NULL, PART DECIMAL NOT NULL, PRODUCT_NAM NVARCHAR(500), SCIENTIFIC_NAME NVARCHAR(500), PART_NAME NVARCHAR(500), CONSTRAINT PK_V_SEM_PRODUCT_NAMES_MOD PRIMARY KEY (PRODUCT_NAME_ID));
INSERT INTO DB.PEST.V_SEM_PRODUCT_NAMES_MOD (PRODUCT_CODE, PRODUCT_NAME_ID, LANGUAGE_CD, PART, PRODUCT_NAM, SCIENTIFIC_NAME, PART_NAME) VALUES ('0130011', 9950, 'LV', 1, 'ZIEDU SĒKLAS', NULL, NULL);

INSERT INTO DB.PEST.V_SEM_PRODUCT_NAMES_MOD (PRODUCT_CODE, PRODUCT_NAME_ID, LANGUAGE_CD, PART, PRODUCT_NAM, SCIENTIFIC_NAME, PART_NAME) VALUES ('0130012', 9951, 'LV', 2, 'AUGU SĒKLAS UN AUGĻI', NULL, NULL);
SELECT * FROM DB.PEST.V_SEM_PRODUCT_NAMES_MOD WHERE PRODUCT_NAM LIKE '%SĒKLAS%';
UPDATE DB.PEST.V_SEM_PRODUCT_NAMES_MOD SET PRODUCT_NAM = 'ATJAUNINĀTS SĒKLA' WHERE PRODUCT_NAME_ID = 9950;
key_bug_features: Insertion of Unicode text with non-Latin characters
